behaviors.description: The clause extractor correctly identifies all key terms (parties, dates, obligations, penalties) present in a contract.
behaviors.justification: The clause extractor is an AI model performing semantic information extraction. Different LLM runs may extract different subsets of terms based on prompt interpretation and model inference, especially for indirect phrasing. Mocking would hide failures where the real LLM misses terms. Only real LLM calls expose actual extraction quality failures: semantic understanding gaps, domain language misrecognition, indirect reference handling.
behaviors.test_description: Test the real clause extractor on contracts containing all four key term categories (parties, dates, obligations, penalties) with ground-truth annotations. Verify all terms are identified. Run multiple times to assess consistency. Catch failures in: semantic understanding, domain-specific language recognition, indirect term references, missing obligations, hallucinations.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Test description requires real LLM calls to expose semantic understanding failures; mocks would always pass.

behaviors.description: The risk assessor flags language that violates the configured risk policy, including indirect and euphemistic phrasing.
behaviors.justification: Risk detection requires semantic understanding of how euphemistic language maps to policy violations. The LLM must interpret both the policy constraints and contract phrasing to identify matches. Different runs may flag different euphemisms based on model inference. A mock would always return programmed 'correct' flags, hiding failures where the real LLM misses subtle risk indicators or misapplies policy rules.
behaviors.test_description: Test the real risk assessor on contracts with indirect/euphemistic language (e.g., 'accelerated payment terms' for penalties, 'mutual cost recovery' for penalties). Verify all policy violations are flagged. Run multiple times. Catch failures in: euphemism detection, policy misinterpretation, semantic understanding gaps, incomplete clause analysis, context-dependent risk classification.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Test requires real LLM to detect nuanced language patterns; mocks cannot expose semantic understanding failures.

behaviors.description: The compliance checker correctly determines whether the contract meets all applicable regulatory requirements for the configured jurisdiction.
behaviors.justification: Compliance determination requires the LLM to reason about jurisdiction-specific regulations and apply them to contract language. Different LLM runs may apply regulations differently based on context and model knowledge. Mocking compliance results hides real failures: the LLM misinterprets a requirement, misses a jurisdiction-specific rule, or applies the wrong standard. Only real LLM calls verify actual compliance reasoning.
behaviors.test_description: Test the real compliance checker on contracts for multiple jurisdictions with ground-truth compliance status (pass/fail with reasoning). Verify correct determination including jurisdiction-specific requirements. Run multiple times to assess consistency. Catch failures in: regulatory knowledge gaps, requirement misinterpretation, jurisdiction-specific logic errors, incomplete requirement coverage.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Test requires real LLM reasoning about regulations; mocks cannot expose failures in regulatory understanding or jurisdiction-specific application.

behaviors.description: The merge step preserves conflicting assessments from different reviewers with clear attribution when they disagree on the same clause.
behaviors.justification: Merge logic is deterministic and independent of reviewer quality. Conflicting assessments can be injected via test data (mocked results) to verify the merge handles them correctly. The failure mode is a logic bug in combination logic, not a classification error. No LLM call is needed. The test validates: 'given these conflicting inputs, does merge produce the correct output with both assessments and attribution?'
behaviors.test_description: Create test data with two reviewers providing conflicting assessments on the same clause (e.g., extractor: 'standard clause', risk assessor: 'high risk'). Call the merge function with these mocked inputs. Verify the output contains both assessments with clear attribution to each reviewer. Test edge cases: three-way conflicts (all reviewers disagree), partial conflicts (one reviewer silent), empty/null assessments, duplicate flagging.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Test uses mocked reviewer results and verifies pure merge logic; no I/O or LLM calls needed to catch merge failures.

behaviors.description: The full pipeline dispatches to all three parallel reviewers, collects results, and merges them into a unified report without losing any findings.
behaviors.justification: This is a multi-step agentic pipeline: dispatch to three parallel LLM reviewers → collect results in parallel → merge. Each reviewer call is Agentic (LLM-dependent). The orchestration across multiple reviewers in parallel/sequence is Workflow scale. Real contracts and real LLM calls are required because: (1) problem specifies 'real contract', (2) 'without losing findings' requires real reviewers to produce findings that can be lost, (3) parallel execution timing issues and reviewer failures only appear with real I/O. Mocked reviewers would hide LLM non-determinism, timeout scenarios, and orchestration failures under load.
behaviors.test_description: Run the complete pipeline on real contracts using real LLM calls to all three reviewers in parallel. Verify: all three reviewers are dispatched correctly, all results are collected (none lost during parallel collection), the merged report contains findings from all three reviewers, no data loss during merge, report schema is valid. Run multiple times to catch non-deterministic failures in orchestration. Catch failures in: reviewer timeout/crash handling, incomplete result collection, parallelization bugs, per-reviewer findings lost in merge, schema mismatches between reviewers, race conditions.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Test requires real parallel LLM calls and real contract document processing; System-level mocking would miss LLM failures and non-deterministic orchestration bugs.
